name: Prefab Site Office
description:
A prefab site office is a factory-built office for a project site — made from insulated panels on a steel frame and delivered as a finished, wired unit ready to use. SAMAN builds it as a 20 × 10 ft steel-and-wood structure from ₹2,15,000. It is not a shipping container and not a rough site shed; it is a panel-built office.

What a Prefab Site Office Is — Panel-Built and Delivered Whole

Search results mix "prefab site office" and "prefabricated site office" — they are the short and long form of the same product, and this page covers both. What makes it prefab is the method: the walls, roof and floor are manufactured in our Bangalore and Greater Noida factories and joined together, so the office arrives as a complete unit rather than being built up on site. For a project manager, that means a working office on day one instead of weeks of masonry.

What You Get and What It Costs — One Configured Office, Not a Per-Sq-Ft Estimate

A prefab site office at work on a live project site.

Most suppliers quote a prefab site office by the square foot, which hides the real total until you ask. SAMAN gives one figure: ₹2,15,000 for the configured 20 × 10 × 8.6 ft unit (regular ₹2,35,000) — 200 sq ft of usable floor. That covers the steel frame, insulated sandwich-panel walls and roof, internal electrical wiring with switchboard, a finished floor, a door and windows. It seats a site engineer and a small team with room for drawings and a meeting corner. A larger footprint or a toilet attachment is available as a factory-built site office to your specification.

How Fast a Prefab Site Office Goes Up — Setup Time by Size

Working condition within a day or two of delivery.

Because the unit is built in the factory while your base is being prepared, on-site time is short. A single 200 sq ft office like this one is delivered as one piece and is standing and wired within a day or two of reaching a level, compacted base. A larger multi-room office — joined from several panels and modules — takes a few days more to assemble and connect. Either way, a quick-build site office reaches working condition in a fraction of the time a built-up room needs, with no curing and no weather delays.

Prefab Panel, Container, or Porta Cabin — Which Site-Office Format

Light enough to place fast on highway and infrastructure sites.

SAMAN builds site offices in three formats. The right one depends on how heavy your site is, how long the project runs, and which term you search by:

Prefab site office (this page) Container site office Porta cabin site office
Built from Insulated panels + steel frame ISO shipping container MS-frame porta cabin
Weight / feel Light, room-like Heavy, secure shell Medium, standard cabin
Best for Fast, flexible-size offices Long 18–36 month deployments Standard contractor offices
From ₹2,15,000 container-based cabin-based

If your project runs for years or needs a hard, lockable shell, our container site office options suit better. If you simply want a standard MS contractor cabin, our porta cabin site office covers that. This page is the prefabricated site office in panel form — lightest to place and quickest to configure to an unusual size.

Reusing the Office Across Projects — Why Contractors Buy Prefab

Bolts apart and moves to the next project — the reason contractors buy.

The reason contractors buy rather than rent is reuse. A prefab site office bolts together and comes apart cleanly, so the same unit moves to your next project on a flatbed and goes back up — no demolition, no write-off. Across three or four deployments the cost per project falls well below repeated rentals or built-up site rooms, and you keep an asset on your books. For undeveloped or remote sites, the light panel build is easier to transport and place than a container.

From Site Office to Site Accommodation — SAMAN's Prefab Range

Part of SAMAN's prefab range — from site offices to crew accommodation.

This prefab site office sits in SAMAN's wider prefabricated house range, which also covers bunkhouses and site accommodation for your crew. If what you actually need is a business or corporate workspace rather than a project-site office, our portable office cabin range is built for that instead.

Prefab Site Office — FAQs

What is a prefab site office?

A prefab site office is a factory-built office for a project site, made from insulated panels on a steel frame and delivered as a finished, wired unit — ready to use far faster than a built-up room.

How long does it take to set up a prefab site office?

A single 200 sq ft unit is placed and wired within a day or two of reaching a prepared base. Larger multi-room offices take a few days to assemble.

How much does a prefab site office cost?

SAMAN's 20 × 10 ft prefab site office is ₹2,15,000 (regular ₹2,35,000). Larger sizes, a toilet, or extra fitout change the figure.

What's the difference between a prefab site office and a container site office?

A prefab site office is panel-built and lighter, easy to size and place; a container site office is a heavy ISO container shell suited to long deployments and high security.

Get a Free Quote for Your Prefab Site Office

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
